title: 7 Top Herbal Remedies for Jaw Clenching Relief

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valeranone is good for jaw clenching because it helps to relax muscles and reduce stress, which can cause tension in the jaw and lead to clenching.
Valerenic acid helps to reduce jaw clenching by relaxing the muscles in the jaw, thereby reducing tension and pain caused by teeth grinding and clenching.

Isovaleric acid is good for jaw clenching because it may help relax muscles and reduce stress, which can contribute to jaw clenching and other teeth grinding issues by releasing tension in the jaw and face area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tincture can help with jaw clenching because it has a calming effect on the body. The tincture comes from the root of the valerian plant, which is known for its ability to relax muscles and reduce anxiety. When you clench your jaw, it's often due to stress or anxiety, and the valerian root helps to ease these feelings. It works by reducing the production of stress hormones in the body, such as cortisol, which can contribute to muscle tension. By calming the nervous system, the tincture can help to relax the jaw muscles, making it easier to release the clench. This can lead to a reduction in pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching, such as headaches and facial tension. The tincture can also help improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted by jaw clenching, further reducing the symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures that help with jaw clenching, you will need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, you will need to gather dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, which can be purchased at a local health food store or online. Next, combine one cup of the dried leaves with two cups of 80-proof vodka or another high-proof spirit in a clean glass jar. Make sure the leaves are completely covered by the liquid, and then seal the jar tightly. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the liquid extract the beneficial compounds from the leaves. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Your Ginkgo biloba tincture is now ready to use - simply take a few drops under the tongue as needed, ideally when you feel yourself clenching your jaw. As with any herbal remedy, be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before using Ginkgo biloba tincture, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking prescription medications.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal tinctures for jaw clenching

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for jaw clenching because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the face, which can ease tension and relieve symptoms of jaw clenching, also known as bruxism.
Quercetin is good for jaw clenching because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce muscle tension and relax the jaw muscles.

Polyphenols are good for jaw clenching because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and tension in the jaw muscles, making it easier to relax and release the habit of clenching.

Tannins are good for jaw clenching because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce muscle tension and relax the jaw muscles, easing the pain and discomfort associated with teeth grinding and jaw clenching.
Kaempferol is good for jaw clenching because it has strong antioxidant properties that help reduce muscle inflammation and spasms, thereby easing jaw tension and promoting relaxation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tincture is a natural remedy that can help with jaw clenching. The main reason for this is that it has a sedative effect on the nervous system. When we clench our jaws, it's often due to anxiety or stress, which can be caused by an overactive nervous system. Passiflora incarnata tincture works by calming down the nervous system, making it easier to relax and reduce muscle tension. This includes the muscles in the jaw, which can become strained and sore from clenching. By relaxing these muscles, Passiflora incarnata tincture can help reduce jaw clenching and alleviate the associated pain and discomfort. The tincture also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease pain in the jaw area. This makes it a potentially effective natural remedy for managing jaw clenching and related issues.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tincture that helps with jaw clenching, start by gathering dried Ginkgo biloba leaves. You can buy these from a health store or online. Fill a clean glass jar about 1/4 full with the dried leaves. Then, add a high-proof spirit like vodka or rum, making sure the leaves are completely covered. The ratio is usually 1 part herbs to 2 parts spirit. Close the jar tightly and place it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This is called maceration and it helps the spirit extract the herbs' active ingredients. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle. Discard the solids and use the tincture as directed. For jaw clenching, take 20-30 drops of the tincture in water 2-3 times a day. Note: Always consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any medical conditions or take medications.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for jaw clenching because it has a relaxing effect on the body, which can help reduce muscle tension and pain in the jaw, thereby easing the symptoms of jaw clenching.
Linalyl acetate is good for jaw clenching because it has a calming effect that helps reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of teeth grinding and jaw tension.

Rosmarinic acid is good for jaw clenching because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce muscle tension and relax jaw muscles, thereby providing relief from jaw clenching and associated pain.
Lavandulol is good for jaw clenching because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce tension and relax the muscles in the jaw, helping to prevent and alleviate jaw clenching.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tincture is a natural remedy for jaw clenching, also known as bruxism. This condition causes people to tightly clench their jaws, often during sleep, which can lead to headaches, pain in the face and neck, and even damage to the teeth. The tincture, made from the flowers of the lavender plant, contains calming compounds that can help relax the muscles in the jaw and face. When taken internally, it can reduce anxiety and stress, which are common causes of jaw clenching. The tincture's calming effects can also help improve sleep quality, which is essential for people who clench their jaws during sleep. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Lavandula angustifolia may help reduce pain and swelling in the jaw and face.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures that help with jaw clenching, you will need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and a clean glass jar. Start by measuring out one part of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and mixing it with one part of vodka in the glass jar, making sure the leaves are completely covered by the vodka. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for about two weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the vodka extract the beneficial properties from the leaves. After two weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Transfer the liquid to a smaller bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. The Ginkgo biloba tincture can be taken as needed by adding a few drops to water or another liquid, and some people find it helpful for reducing jaw clenching by relaxing the muscles in the face and jaw.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Avenanthramides are good for jaw clenching because they have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and tension in the jaw muscles, thus alleviating the symptoms of jaw clenching.
Saponins are good for jaw clenching because they help relax muscles and reduce inflammation in the jaw, which can help to ease the pain and tension caused by clenching.

Flavonoids are good for jaw clenching because they help reduce inflammation and relax tense muscles, making it easier to manage and alleviate the discomfort caused by teeth grinding and jaw clenching.
Polyphenols are good for jaw clenching because they reduce inflammation and relax muscles, which helps to ease tension and pain in the jaw caused by clenching.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tincture is a natural remedy that can help with jaw clenching. It contains a compound called avens that has a calming effect on the body. When taken internally, it can reduce stress and anxiety, which are common causes of jaw clenching. This tincture works by slowing down the nervous system, allowing the muscles in the jaw to relax. As a result, the habit of clenching the jaw, also known as bruxism, decreases. Avena sativa tincture is also known to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce pain and discomfort caused by jaw clenching. Furthermore, it can help regulate sleep patterns, which is often disrupted by jaw clenching. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, Avena sativa tincture can provide relief from jaw clenching and its associated symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures that help with jaw clenching, start by gathering fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and roots. You'll need a clean glass jar with a lid, a solvent like vodka or glycerin, and a clean glass measuring cup. Begin by measuring out the Ginkgo biloba leaves and roots, using a ratio of one part herb to two parts solvent. For example, if you're using one ounce of Ginkgo biloba, use two ounces of vodka. Next, combine the herb and solvent in the glass jar, making sure the herb is completely covered. Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This process is called "steeping," and it allows the active compounds in the Ginkgo biloba to infuse into the solvent. After the steeping period, strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken sublingually (under the tongue) to help alleviate jaw clenching and promote relaxation.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for jaw clenching because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce muscle tension and relax the jaw muscles.
Shogaol is good for jaw clenching because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce muscle tension and pain in the jaw, making it easier to relax and release tension.
Tannins are good for jaw clenching because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce tension and ease pain in the jaw, jaw muscles, and temporomandibular joint.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural herb that can help with jaw clenching. The tincture form of ginger is a liquid extract that contains the herb's active compounds. These compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and tension in the jaw. When you clench your jaw, it can cause your muscles to become tight and inflamed, leading to pain and discomfort. Ginger tincture can help relax these muscles, reducing the pain and tension associated with jaw clenching. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger can also help reduce swelling and inflammation in the jaw, which can contribute to pain and discomfort. Additionally, ginger tincture can help calm the nervous system, which can contribute to jaw clenching. By reducing stress and promoting relaxation, ginger tincture can help alleviate the symptoms of jaw clenching, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba tincture that helps with jaw clenching, you'll need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. Start by purchasing or foraging Ginkgo biloba leaves, then dry them completely in a low-temperature oven or with a food dehydrator. Once the leaves are dry, chop them into small pieces and fill a clean glass jar about 1/4 full. Next, pour a solvent like vodka or glycerin over the leaves, making sure they're completely covered. The general ratio is to use 1 part dried leaves to 2-3 parts solvent, but you can adjust this to your liking. Seal the jar tightly and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This process is called maceration, and it helps the active ingredients from the Ginkgo biloba leaves infuse into the solvent. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Ginkgo biloba tincture is now ready to use, and you can take it orally by placing a few drops under your tongue or mixing it with water.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, is a herb that has been used for centuries to calm the mind and body. The tincture made from this herb has been found to be effective in reducing jaw clenching, also known as bruxism. This is because the active ingredients in lemon balm, such as rosmarinic acid and eugenol, have a soothing effect on the muscles in the face and jaw. When these muscles are tense, it can cause the jaw to clench, leading to discomfort and pain. The tincture of Melissa officinalis helps to relax these muscles, reducing the frequency and severity of jaw clenching episodes. Additionally, lemon balm has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can also contribute to the reduction of jaw clenching. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, Melissa officinalis tincture can provide relief from the symptoms of bruxism and help to prevent further damage to the teeth and jaw.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba tincture that helps with jaw clenching, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: Ginkgo biloba leaves, vodka or a neutral spirit, and a clean glass jar. First, dry the Ginkgo biloba leaves thoroughly in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them. Next, fill the glass jar about a quarter full with the dried leaves, and then fill the rest of the jar with vodka or the neutral spirit, making sure the leaves are completely covered. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for about two weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the herb infuse into the liquid. After two weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid tincture. Your Ginkgo biloba tincture is now ready to use: take 20-30 drops of the tincture, mixed with water, 2-3 times a day to help alleviate jaw clenching and promote relaxation.

When you're stressed, your muscles tense up, including the ones in your jaw. Ginkgo biloba tinctures can reduce the production of stress hormones like cortisol, which helps to relax your muscles. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba is known to improve cognitive function, which can help to manage stress and anxiety that often lead to jaw clenching.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Ginkgo biloba herbal tincture that helps with jaw clenching, you'll need dried Ginkgo biloba leaves and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. Start by measuring and mixing 1 part of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 parts of the solvent in a clean glass jar. Make sure the leaves are completely covered by the solvent. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2 to 3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This process is called maceration, where the solvent extracts the beneficial compounds from the leaves. After the maceration period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. You can now use the Ginkgo biloba tincture as needed by mixing a few drops in water or taking it as directed by a healthcare professional. This herbal remedy may help reduce jaw clenching and alleviate related symptoms.

intro_desc: Jaw clenching, also known as bruxism, can be a painful and debilitating condition that affects millions of people worldwide. It's not just a minor annoyance, as it can lead to teeth grinding, facial pain, and even headaches. However, there's a natural solution that can help alleviate this issue - herbal tinctures. Specifically, tinctures made from Valeriana officinalis and Passiflora incarnata have been shown to be effective in reducing jaw clenching and related symptoms.  Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, is a natural relaxant that has been used for centuries to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. When taken as a tincture, it can help reduce muscle tension and anxiety, which are common triggers for jaw clenching. By relaxing the muscles in the face and jaw, valerian root tincture can help prevent teeth grinding and facial pain.  Passiflora incarnata, or passionflower, is another herb that has been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. It contains compounds that have a sedative effect, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that may be contributing to jaw clenching. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, passionflower tincture can help alleviate symptoms of bruxism.  It's essential to address jaw clenching issues because they can have a significant impact on daily life. Chronic teeth grinding and facial pain can lead to sleep disturbances, decreased productivity, and even affect relationships. By using herbal tinctures like valerian root and passionflower, individuals can find a natural and effective way to manage their symptoms and improve their overall well-being.
supplementary_best: For jaw clenching, a combination of Valeriana officinalis and Passiflora incarnata tinctures is recommended. Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, is a natural sedative that can help relax the muscles and calm the nervous system. Passiflora incarnata, or passionflower, has a similar effect and can further reduce anxiety and stress, which are common causes of jaw clenching. This combination is effective because it addresses the root causes of the issue from multiple angles. Valeriana officinalis targets the physical tension, while Passiflora incarnata tackles the emotional and psychological factors.
supplementary_avoid: If you have jaw clenching, also known as bruxism, it's best to avoid herbal tinctures that can stimulate or tighten muscles.  One such herb is Yohimbe (Pausinystalia johimbe). Yohimbe can cause muscle tension, which may worsen jaw clenching. This is because Yohimbe can increase adrenaline levels in the body, leading to increased muscle activity.  Another herb to avoid is Ephedra (Ephedra sinica). Ephedra is known to stimulate the nervous system, leading to increased muscle activity and tension. This can exacerbate jaw clenching and make it harder to relax the muscles in the face and jaw.  Ginseng (Panax ginseng) can also be problematic. While it's often used to reduce stress, some people may find it increases anxiety, which can lead to muscle tension and worsen jaw clenching.  Finally, Black Cohosh (Cimicifuga racemosa) can also cause muscle tension. Black Cohosh can stimulate the nervous system and may worsen jaw clenching in some people.  These herbs should be used with caution or avoided if you have jaw clenching, as they may worsen the condition. It's best to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
supplementary_other_remedies: Jaw clenching, also known as bruxism, can be caused by stress and anxiety, but it can also be a result of physical factors like misaligned teeth or a bad bite. One natural remedy that can help with jaw clenching is a warm compress. Applying a warm compress to the jaw area can help relax the muscles, making it easier to release tension. This is because heat increases blood flow, which can help ease muscle cramps and spasms. A warm compress can be made by soaking a cloth in warm water, wringing it out, and applying it to the jaw for a few minutes.  Another remedy is to try a massage. Massaging the jaw area, especially the masseter muscle, can help release tension and relax the muscles. This can be done by a professional massage therapist or by yourself using a foam roller or your fingers. Massaging the jaw area can help break up knots in the muscles and improve circulation, which can help reduce jaw clenching.  Cold or hot packs can be applied to the jaw to relax the muscles and reduce pain. Some people find that cold packs work better, as the cold can numb the area and reduce pain, while others prefer hot packs, which can help relax the muscles.  Some people find that changing their diet can also help with jaw clenching. Eating softer foods, like yogurt or scrambled eggs, can put less strain on the jaw muscles, which can help reduce clenching. Avoiding hard or chewy foods, like nuts or candy, can also help.  Exercising the jaw muscles can also be helpful. Exercises like opening the mouth as wide as possible and then releasing, or moving the jaw from side to side, can help strengthen the muscles and improve coordination. This can help reduce tension and clenching.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience jaw clenching, also known as bruxism, often have other health issues. For example, they may have headaches and migraines due to the constant grinding and tension in the jaw muscles. This can be relieved with feverfew, a herbal remedy that reduces inflammation and eases pain.  Tension in the jaw can also lead to earaches and tinnitus, or ringing in the ears, as the nerves in the face and head become irritated. Ginkgo biloba can help alleviate these symptoms by improving blood flow to the nerves and reducing inflammation.  Bruxism can also cause neck and shoulder pain, as the tension in the jaw radiates to the surrounding muscles. Valerian root can help relax these muscles and reduce pain, promoting a good night's sleep.  Additionally, people with bruxism may experience anxiety and stress, which can trigger jaw clenching. Passionflower can help calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, breaking the cycle of bruxism.  Finally, bruxism can also lead to dental problems, such as tooth wear and gum recession, as the constant grinding wears down the teeth and puts pressure on the gums. Licorice root can help soothe and protect the gums, while slippery elm can help protect the teeth and reduce inflammation in the mouth.  Feverfew, ginkgo biloba, valerian root, passionflower, licorice root, and slippery elm are all herbs that have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms related to bruxism, and may be worth exploring as a natural solution.
